Netherlands.status: Safe — advances with W or D. Even with a loss, advances if JPN doesn't win vs SWE.

Tunisia.status: ELIMINATED — no mathematical path to advancement

matches.note: 1H goal by Rekik (43'), rest of match 0 Tunisia SOT
Complete shutout — 0 SOT in entire match

TUN.CRITICAL: Tunisia SOT collapsed from 2.8 pre-WC average to 1.0 WC average. 0 SOT vs Japan. 2H goals = 0 in both WC matches.

context_flags: NED_88pct_FAVORITE: Massive mismatch. Classic blowout regime.
TUN_ELIMINATED: No motivation beyond pride. Disengage pattern expected (0 SOT vs JPN in GD2).
RULE10: In blowouts, winning team fouls ≥ losing team. NED likely fouls more than TUN.
TUN_2H_COLLAPSE: Tunisia has scored 0 goals in any second half across both WC2026 matches.
TUN_SOT_CRISIS: 0 SOT vs Japan (entire match), 2 SOT vs Sweden. WC avg = 1.0 SOT/game.
SKHIRI_CDM: Defensive midfielder with liquid market at 6.67% SOT — one of lowest player props seen.

questions.text: Will Tunisia commit more fouls than Netherlands?
questions.reasoning: Both teams average exactly 8.0 fouls per game in WC2026. True coin flip on data. RULE10 (blowout: winning team fouls ≥ losing team) suggests NED may actually foul more. No edge. Skip or submit 0.45.

questions.text: Will both teams have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
questions.reasoning: NED almost certain to get 2H SOT (avg 6.5 total/game). Tunisia: 0 SOT in GD2 vs Japan, 2 in GD1 vs Sweden. P(TUN gets ≥1 SOT in 2H) is genuinely low — maybe 35-40%. Tunisia's 2H goal probability is only 24% from market; SOT probability is higher but this team is being shut out. Estimate: 0.38.

questions.text: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets BTTS+OU2.5 Yes/Over = 32.73%. Direct tier-1 market. Trust it.

questions.text: Will Netherlands receive more cards than Tunisia?
questions.reasoning: Most cards market: NED more cards = 23.33%. This is a liquid market and the low figure makes sense: Tunisia had only 1 YC total in 2 WC matches and disengages in blowouts. NED won't need to foul recklessly. The crowd will intuitively think 'NED is the big team that fouls' but the data says otherwise. Strong NO.

questions.text: Will Tunisia score at least 1 goal?
questions.reasoning: BTTS = 38%. P(TUN scores) ≈ 38-40% (BTTS + small chance TUN wins clean). Tunisia did score in GD1 vs Sweden (43'), did not score vs Japan. Market suggests about 38-40% chance. Submit at 0.39.

questions.text: At halftime, will the match be tied?
questions.reasoning: Direct HT result market: Draw = 27.65%. NED has 66.55% probability to be leading at HT. In GD2, NED led 2-0 at HT vs Sweden. Against a weaker Tunisia, NED leading at half is very likely. Trust the liquid market.

questions.text: Will Tunisia score in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets market: TUN 2H goals OU0.5 Over = 24%. Tunisia has scored 0 second-half goals in both WC2026 matches. Rekik's goal vs Sweden was in 43rd minute (1H). 0 goals vs Japan. The blowout pattern (NED comfortably ahead by HT) ensures Tunisia disengages in 2H. This is one of our highest-conviction questions: tier-1 direct market + confirming historical data. Submit at 0.24.

questions.text: Will Ellyes Skhiri have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets LIQUID market: 6.67%. Skhiri is Tunisia's defensive midfielder. Tunisia's TEAM total SOT in WC2026: 2 (GD1, full match) and 0 (GD2, full match). For a CDM to get a SOT when his team is essentially not registering any shots is essentially impossible. We've correctly beaten crowd on Skhiri before: SWE-TUN +10.77 RBP (market 15.83%, crowd 31%). Today the market is even lower (6.67%), the opposition is even better, and Tunisia's recent form is even worse. Submit at market: 0.07. Crowd will drift to 0.25-0.30.

questions.text: Will there be 8 or more total shots on target?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets OU7.5 Over = 61.07%. NED averages 6.5 SOT/game against strong opponents. Against a Tunisia defense that's allowed 4 and 5 goals in two matches, NED's SOT total should exceed their average. 7+ SOT from NED alone is plausible. Tunisia adds some (even if small). YES lean is well-supported by market and data.

questions.text: Will Cody Gakpo score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets LIQUID market: 66.1%. Gakpo has participated in goals in both WC2026 matches. NED expected to score multiple goals (OU2.5 Over = 70%). Tunisia allowed 4 goals to JPN and 5 to SWE. As NED's primary attacker, Gakpo should get involved. Consistent with our prior result: GD2 vs SWE we priced Gakpo SOT at 0.64 (market) and won +4.96 RBP. Trust the liquid market.

strongest_picks: Q8: Skhiri SOT = 0.07 (MAXIMUM CONFIDENCE — liquid market 6.67%, TUN 0 SOT in GD2, CDM role, blowout expected)
Q7: Tunisia scores in 2H = 0.24 (HIGHEST CONFIDENCE — direct market, TUN 0 second-half goals in both WC matches)
Q4: NED more cards = 0.23 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — liquid most_cards market, TUN only 1 total YC in WC2026)
Q6: HT tied = 0.28 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct HT market, NED 66.6% to lead at HT)

Q1_TUN_more_fouls.note: We flagged this as our lowest-confidence pick (true coin flip on equal 8.0/8.0 foul averages) and it cost us. Tunisia, eliminated and getting blown out, fouled more out of frustration/desperation defending — same dynamic as RULE10 blowout fouling, but we didn't apply it to the LOSING team's foul rate.

Q2_both_SOT_2H.note: Biggest single loss of the day. We bet heavily on Tunisia's SOT collapse (0 SOT vs Japan) repeating, but Tunisia got on the board with a 2H SOT/goal against a Netherlands team that had already let up once they were comfortably ahead. The crowd's higher 59% correctly priced in garbage-time space for the loser.

Q7_TUN_scores_2H.note: Tunisia's '0 second-half goals across 2 WC matches' streak broke. Direct market read missed once garbage-time defending opened up — same root cause as Q2.

Q8_Skhiri_SOT.note: Our maximum-confidence pick held — Skhiri's CDM role and Tunisia's team-wide SOT crisis stayed true even as Tunisia broke through elsewhere on the pitch.

Q10_Gakpo_score_or_assist.note: Liquid market (66%) failed for the second time this tournament — same pattern as Balogun's liquid SOT market missing in USA-AUS. Netherlands won comfortably without Gakpo needing to be directly involved in a goal; subs/other scorers carried the scoreline.

key_confirmations: The two biggest losses (Q2, Q7 = -22.98 combined) share one root cause: we modeled Tunisia as a team that simply doesn't generate second-half output, based on their complete shutout vs Japan. But that shutout happened against a disciplined, still-motivated Japan; here, a comfortably-ahead Netherlands likely eased off, handing Tunisia exactly the space/garbage-time chances they lacked in GD2. LESSON: 'team has been shut out' signals are conditional on opponent intensity, not a fixed team trait — re-derive for each specific opponent's likely game-state behavior once ahead.

Q10 (Gakpo) is now the SECOND liquid star-attacker market to fail this tournament (after Balogun in USA-AUS, and again in TUR-USA) — building toward a real pattern: trust liquid 'big name to score/assist' props less when the team is winning comfortably, since the goal/assist can come from a teammate instead.
